behavior_summary: Provides thin event-handler wrappers for a non-solid door object that set up the object context and delegate the real logic to shared DOOR_NS handlers.
Event 0 (TDOOR_NS::look) prepares object info, excludes the current process, then spawns DOOR_NS::00D0 to perform the 'look' (examine) behavior and returns its result.
Event 1 (TDOOR_NS::use) does the same setup, pushes an explicit byte flag value 1, then spawns DOOR_NS::08B4 to perform the 'use' behavior and returns its result.
Event 6 (TDOOR_NS::gotHit) forwards a hit/damage parameter (from BP+0Ah) along with the actor to DOOR_NS::0C82 after setting context and excluding the process.
No global flags are read or written by these wrappers; they exist to forward calls with a required context and exclusion step rather than implement game logic themselves.
notable_control_flow: Each event handler follows the same sequence: push object-info constant -> push actor/context -> call 'set info' -> call 'process exclude' -> spawn the DOOR_NS handler -> pop spawn return into a temp -> return.
Spawn is used to invoke shared DOOR_NS logic; the spawn return value is captured and propagated back to the original caller.
'process exclude' is always invoked immediately after 'set info', indicating a required pre-spawn exclusion/guard step before delegating behavior.
open_questions: What exact engine behavior does 'process exclude' perform (scheduler exclusion, notification suppression, or something else)?
What do the concrete object-info pointer constants (e.g., 0x0208, 0x0207, 0x020C observed in the disassembly) refer to in game data (different subtypes/variants of the door)?
What semantics does the leading byte flag = 1 passed to DOOR_NS::08B4 encode for 'use' (mode, interaction subtype, or caller identity)?
Are these event handlers invoked both by direct player actions and by area triggers (enterFastArea/leaveFastArea), and if so does that change the meaning of the actor parameter?

main_function.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::look (Event 0)
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'look' (examine) interaction on a non-solid door by setting the appropriate object context, excluding the current process, and delegating the actual examine behavior to the shared DOOR_NS::00D0 handler. The wrapper captures and returns the spawn result from the delegated handler.
entry_conditions: Invoked when an actor (player or other process) inspects or 'looks at' the door (Event 0).
Also likely invoked when the actor enters a trigger region associated with the door (enterFastArea semantics inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::look (Event 0)
function_map.role: Prepare object context and delegate 'look' behavior to DOOR_NS::00D0; capture and return spawn retval.

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::use (Event 1)
function_map.role: Prepare object context and delegate 'use' behavior to DOOR_NS::08B4, passing an extra byte flag (1).

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::gotHit (Event 6)
function_map.role: Forward hit/damage notifications to DOOR_NS::0C82 after context setup; passes through hit parameter from the caller.

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::00D0
function_map.role: Shared implementation of door 'look' behavior invoked by the TDOOR_NS wrapper.

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::08B4
function_map.role: Shared implementation of door 'use' behavior; receives a leading byte flag (1) from the wrapper.

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::0C82
function_map.role: Shared implementation of door hit/damage behavior invoked by the wrapper.

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::set info
function_map.role: Engine call to set the current object/actor context for subsequent processing.

function_map.name: U8P_TDOOR_NS::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine call that excludes or marks the current process prior to spawning the delegated handler (guard/cleanup step).

external_calls.purpose: Establish the object info/context so the spawned handler operates on the correct door instance.
Exclude or mark the current process before spawning the DOOR_NS handler (pre-spawn guard).
Delegate the 'look' logic to the shared DOOR_NS::00D0 handler, passing the actor and prepared context; capture and return its retval.
Establish the object context for the use-handler to operate on the correct instance.
Pre-spawn exclusion/guard before delegating to the DOOR_NS use handler.
Delegate 'use' behavior to DOOR_NS::08B4, passing a leading byte flag (1), the actor, and context; capture and return retval.
Set the object context for the hit/damage handler.
Exclude or mark the process before delegating hit logic.
Delegate hit/damage handling to the shared DOOR_NS::0C82, passing the hit parameter and actor along with context; capture and return retval.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides thin event-handler wrappers for a non-solid door object that set up the object context and delegate the real logic to shared DOOR_NS handlers.
